item.title: The Greatness Polarity Index
item.description: It is often said that love and hate are two sides of the same coin. You can’t have one without the other. The yin to another’s yang. Being a football fan can be the same way at times. We often say to ourselves, “our great [one side of the ball] is being wasted by a trash [other side of the ball].” To that, I posit that having a top-tier [one side of the ball] and an [other side of the ball] that you may find at the end of the driveway during spring cleaning season are two different forms of greatness.

item.title: Hello R Markdown
item.description: R Markdown This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com. You can embed an R code chunk like this: summary(cars) ## speed dist ## Min. : 4.0 Min. : 2.00 ## 1st Qu.:12.0 1st Qu.: 26.00 ## Median :15.0 Median : 36.00 ## Mean :15.4 Mean : 42.98 ## 3rd Qu.

item.title: About
item.description: I started this project after getting laid off from a job and hoping to find a new career path. I’ve always been an avid sports fan and have been particularly interested in the strange parts of sports - the parts where the best athletes in the world look just like you and me. So I set out to see if I could quantify the strangest parts of sports. I hope you’ll join me on this journey.

<!DOCTYPE html> All Team's GPI - Ben DeMouth About GitHub Twitter 1 min read All Team's GPI 2024-04-15 <link href=“/2024/04/15/all-teams-gpi/index_files/crosstalk/css/crosstalk.min.css” rel=“stylesheet” /> <link href=“/2024/04/15/all-teams-gpi/index_files/plotly-htmlwidgets-css/plotly-htmlwidgets.css” rel=“stylesheet” /> I recently wrote an article on the top-10 teams in a stat I invented called the Greatness Polarity Index. If you were wondering how your team stacked up, check out this interactive graph below. Made with
